stim_key.Description: N400Stimset code (i.e. stimulus order in Toffolo et al. 2022)

stim_file.Description: name of the auditory stimulus file presented

known_word.Description: the known word (via the online word database) that the sentence stimulus was based off of

stim_dur(s).Description: the duration of the entire stimulus (sentence)

target_onset(s).Description: the onset of the target word within a stimulus (final word at the end of a sentence)

target_end.Description: the end of the target word within a stimulus

target_dur.Description: the duration of the target word within a stimulus

time-quarter_div.Description: what quarter of the experiment was the stimulus played

order-group_div.Description: what order was the stimulus played
order-group_div.1: Congruent stimulus, where the congruent pair was presented before the incongruent pair
order-group_div.2: Incongruent stimulus, where the congruent pair was presented before the incongruent pair
order-group_div.3: Congruent stimulus, where the incongruent pair was presented before the congruent pair
order-group_div.4: Incongruent stimulus, where the incongruent pair was presented before the congruent pair

cloze-probability%_div.Description: cloze probability scores, or the % of the surveyed participants that responded with the anticipated congruent ending word

linguistic-group_div.Description: subdivision of stimuli based on syntactic error between a congruent and incongruent stimulus pair
linguistic-group_div.1: no syntactic error (congruent and incongruent expectation type matches)
linguistic-group_div.2: syntactic number error (expecting a plural noun but incongruent ending was a singular noun, or vice versa)
linguistic-group_div.3: syntactic adjective error (expecting an adjective but incongruent ending was a noun/verb, or vice versa)
linguistic-group_div.4: syntactic verb error (expecting a verb but incongruent ending was a noun, or vice versa)
linguistic-group_div.5: misc (stimulus pair contained cultural references, or the incongruent ending did not match congruent ending in syllable number, contained hyphenated phrases, or could be congruent)

linguistic-group_reasoning.Description: specific reasoning for linguistic division category
